the re-export pattern itself is benign.\n- No untrusted data, environment variables, or external inputs are involved.\n- No hardcoded secrets, credentials, or malicious payloads are evident.\n\n---\n\n### Review of Each Report:\n\n**Report 1:**  \n- Purpose: Re-export utilities — benign.  \n- Sources/Sinks/Flows: No malicious data flow.  \n- Anomalies: No suspicious behavior, source maps are typical.  \n- **Conclusion:** Benign, no security risk.  \n- **Scores:** malware=0, obfuscated=0, risk=0 — appropriate.\n\n**Report 2:**  \n- Purpose: Re-export functions/objects — benign.  \n- Analysis: Standard pattern, no malicious intent.  \n- **Conclusion:** No malicious behavior.  \n- **Scores:** malware=0, obfuscated=0, risk=0 — appropriate.\n\n**Report 3:**  \n- Purpose: Re-export for module consolidation — benign.  \n- Analysis: Same pattern, no signs of malicious code.  \n- **Conclusion:** No malicious activity.  \n- **Scores:** malware=0, obfuscated=0, risk=0 — appropriate.\n\n**Report 4:**  \n- Purpose: Re-export module exports — benign.  \n- Analysis: Typical pattern, no suspicious behavior.  \n- **Conclusion:** No malicious intent.  \n- Scores: malware=0, obfuscated=0, risk=0 — appropriate.\n\n**Report 5:**  \n- Purpose: Re-export for easier imports — benign.  \n- Analysis: Standard pattern, no malicious behavior.  \n- Slightly lower confidence (0.9) and a small security risk score (0.1) due to the source map comment.  \n- **Conclusion:** Still benign;
